capabilities.name: Bill of Materials (BOM)
capabilities.description: Structured definition of the components, quantities, and assembly relationships that make up a manufactured product. Foundation for every other manufacturing function.

requirements.text: Support multi-level BOM structure with parent/child/sub-assembly hierarchy to represent complex product compositions
proxy_refs: Multi-level BOM with parent/child/sub-assembly hierarchy

requirements.text: Define component quantities with unit-of-measure and scrap/yield factors for accurate material planning
proxy_refs: Component quantities with unit-of-measure and scrap factor

requirements.text: Manage alternate and substitute components with configurable substitution rules and precedence
proxy_refs: Alternate / substitute components with substitution rules

requirements.text: Support phantom sub-assemblies that explode through to lower levels without creating discrete inventory
proxy_refs: Phantom sub-assemblies (explode-through components for lean production)

requirements.text: Capture reference designators to specify component placement and location on assemblies
proxy_refs: Reference designators (which component goes where on the assembly)

requirements.text: Apply effectivity dates to control when component changes take effect (in-date and out-date)
proxy_refs: Effectivity dates for component changes (in/out by date)

requirements.text: Distinguish between Engineering BOM and Manufacturing BOM to support design vs. production workflows
proxy_refs: Engineering BOM vs Manufacturing BOM distinction

requirements.text: Calculate standard cost rollup from component costs to finished-good level
proxy_refs: Cost rollup from component costs to finished-good standard cost

requirements.text: Generate where-used reports to identify all BOMs and products that reference a given component
proxy_refs: Where-used report (find all BOMs that reference a given component)

features.name: BOM Structure & Hierarchy
features.description: Multi-level parent-child relationships, sub-assemblies, and phantom components for complex product composition.

features.name: Component Definition
features.description: Component quantities, units-of-measure, scrap factors, reference designators, and alternate/substitute rules.

features.name: BOM Variants & Effectivity
features.description: Engineering vs. Manufacturing BOMs, effectivity date control, and design-to-production workflow support.

features.name: Costing & Traceability
features.description: Standard cost rollup from components to finished goods and where-used component traceability.

capabilities.name: Routings, Operations & Work Centers
capabilities.description: Defines the sequence of operations required to produce an item, the work centers each operation runs on, and the standard times for setup, run, and teardown.

requirements.text: Routing defines an ordered sequence of operations required to manufacture or assemble a product

requirements.text: Work center master data includes capacity, cost rate, and efficiency metrics

requirements.text: Each operation captures setup time, run time per piece, and teardown time
proxy_refs: Setup time, run time per piece, and teardown time

requirements.text: Routing supports parallel and alternate operation paths for flexible manufacturing
proxy_refs: Parallel and alternate operation paths

requirements.text: Multiple routing versions can be maintained with effectivity dates to manage changes over time
proxy_refs: Routing versions with effectivity dates

requirements.text: Operations link to BOM components consumed or produced at that manufacturing step

requirements.text: Work instructions, technical drawings, and standard worksheets can be attached to each operation
proxy_refs: Standard worksheet / work instruction attached to each operation

imda_refs.text: A source of information on the sequence of processes involved to manufacture or assemble a product (e.g. technical drawing, Bill of Material (BOM), materials required and manufacturing process such as tools, equipment, instructions used to build the product

imda_refs.text: A source of information on the sequence of processes involved to manufacture or assemble a product (e.g. technical drawing, Bill of Material (BOM), materials required and manufacturing process such as tools, equipment, instructions used to build the product

features.name: Operation Definition

capabilities.name: Work Orders & Job Cards
capabilities.description: Create, schedule, execute, and close production work orders against BOMs and routings — the central operational artifact of a discrete manufacturing ERP.

capabilities.name: Material Requirements Planning (MRP)
capabilities.description: Net-requirements explosion from demand (sales orders + forecast) through BOMs and inventory policies, generating planned purchase and production orders to meet demand without stock-outs or excess.

requirements.text: Aggregate demand from sales orders, forecasts, and stock transfers into a consolidated demand schedule
proxy_refs: Demand aggregation from sales orders, forecasts, and stock transfers

requirements.text: Perform multi-level BOM explosion to calculate net requirements, netting against on-hand inventory and on-order quantities
proxy_refs: Multi-level BOM explosion net of on-hand and on-order quantities

requirements.text: Generate planned purchase orders for externally sourced items based on net requirements
proxy_refs: Planned order generation for purchased items and manufactured items

requirements.text: Generate planned production orders for internally manufactured items based on net requirements
proxy_refs: Planned order generation for purchased items and manufactured items

requirements.text: Apply lot-sizing rules including fixed lot, lot-for-lot, economic order quantity, and period of supply methods
proxy_refs: Lot-size rules (fixed, lot-for-lot, economic, period of supply)

requirements.text: Apply safety-stock and reorder-point policies per item to protect against demand and supply variability
proxy_refs: Safety-stock and reorder-point policies per item

requirements.text: Offset planned order dates by supplier or manufacturing lead times to calculate required start dates
proxy_refs: Lead-time offsetting for start-date calculation

requirements.text: Generate MRP exception messages identifying orders to expedite, defer, or cancel based on schedule changes
proxy_refs: MRP exception messages (expedite, defer, cancel, release)

proxy_refs: What-if MRP run for scenario testing

requirements.text: Provide visibility of dependent demand traceability from finished goods through all component levels to raw materials
proxy_refs: Dependent demand visibility from finished good to raw material

features.name: Scenario Analysis
features.description: Run what-if MRP simulations with alternative demand, supply, or policy parameters.

capabilities.name: Production Scheduling & Capacity Planning
capabilities.description: Assign work orders to work centers across a time horizon, respecting finite capacity and operation sequence — distinct from MRP which generates orders without regard to capacity.

requirements.text: Support both infinite and finite capacity scheduling modes to allow planners to choose between unconstrained and resource-constrained scheduling approaches.
proxy_refs: Infinite and finite capacity scheduling modes

requirements.text: Provide visual work center load display (Gantt chart or resource timeline) showing scheduled operations and available capacity.
proxy_refs: Work center load view (Gantt or resource chart)

requirements.text: Enable forward scheduling from operation start date and backward scheduling from due date to support both push and pull planning strategies.
proxy_refs: Forward scheduling from start date, backward from due date

requirements.text: Allow interactive rescheduling via drag-and-drop with real-time conflict and constraint violation detection.
proxy_refs: Drag-and-drop rescheduling with conflict detection

requirements.text: Generate capacity utilization reports and identify bottleneck work centers limiting throughput.
proxy_refs: Capacity utilisation reports and bottleneck identification

requirements.text: Support what-if scenario modeling to evaluate alternative scheduling plans before commitment.
proxy_refs: What-if scheduling scenarios

requirements.text: Respect calendar exceptions including holidays, shift patterns, and planned maintenance downtime when calculating available capacity.
proxy_refs: Calendar exceptions (holidays, shifts, planned downtime)

requirements.text: Apply finite-capacity sequencing rules with setup-time minimization to reduce changeover losses and improve work center efficiency.
proxy_refs: Finite-capacity sequencing with setup-time minimisation

features.name: Interactive Scheduling & Optimization
features.description: Enable drag-and-drop rescheduling with constraint detection and sequencing rule application.

features.name: Calendar & Constraint Management
features.description: Respect shift patterns, holidays, maintenance windows, and other calendar exceptions in capacity calculations.

features.name: Scenario Planning & Analysis
features.description: Model alternative scheduling plans and evaluate impact before commitment.

capabilities.description: The solution works on phones, tablets, and desktops. Universal requirement — PSG asks every vendor "is this cloud and multi-device accessible?" as the first question on every sol cat. Factory supervisors and operators need mobile access to production data.

requirements.text: Solution is accessible via web browser on phones, tablets, and desktop computers without requiring local installation
proxy_refs: Usable on phone, tablet, and desktop
Cloud-hosted or browser-based — no desktop install needed

requirements.text: User interface automatically adapts to different screen sizes and device orientations
proxy_refs: Responsive design that works on all screen sizes

requirements.text: Users can view production schedules and real-time shop floor status from mobile devices
proxy_refs: Access production schedules and shop floor status from a mobile device

requirements.text: Mobile users receive push notifications or alerts for quality failures and machine downtime events
proxy_refs: Receive alerts for quality failures or machine downtime on mobile

requirements.text: Mobile devices can capture and process barcodes and QR codes using the device camera
proxy_refs: Barcode and QR scanning via mobile camera

features.name: Web Accessibility

features.name: Mobile Capture

capabilities.name: Data Security, Privacy & Consent
capabilities.description: Data hygiene, retention policies, audit trails, and basic access security. Universal — PSG requires every pre-approved vendor to meet PDPA compliance, Cyber Essentials Mark, and VA/PT audits regardless of which module they cover.

requirements.text: Capture and maintain audit trail of PDPA consent for employee and supplier data

requirements.text: Process and execute data deletion requests from employees and business partners

requirements.text: Encrypt data at rest and in transit
proxy_refs: Encrypt data at rest and in transit

requirements.text: Log all access and modifications to production and quality records with user identification
proxy_refs: Log who accessed or changed production and quality records

capabilities.name: User Accounts, Roles & Access Control
capabilities.description: Multiple staff can use the system, each with their own login, role-based permissions, and audit of what they did. Universal — any SME with more than one employee needs this, and PSG requires access control as part of its Cyber Essentials baseline.

requirements.text: Create individual user accounts with unique login credentials for each system user
proxy_refs: Create individual user accounts with unique login credentials

requirements.text: Support user account deactivation when staff leave the organization
